blocks.text:
INT. ARTHUR'S WORKSHOP - NIGHT (MOMENTS TO MIDNIGHT)

An elderly hand, lined with age and tremor, hovers over a workbench. ARTHUR FINCH (70s), a profound sorrow etched on his face, peers through a watchmaker's LOUPE. Before him, the delicate, open mechanism of his late wife's POCKET WATCH.
A majestic GRANDFATHER CLOCK dominates the background. Its ornate face clearly shows the time: TEN SECONDS TO MIDNIGHT. A low, resonant HUM begins to emanate from it.
Arthur holds a minuscule gear with fine-tipped TWEEZERS. His hand shakes, not just from age, but from the immense pressure, the emotional weight of this final, desperate act.
The GRANDFATHER CLOCK CHIMES, deep and sonorous, signaling the first second. Arthur flinches, a bead of sweat tracing a path down his temple. EIGHT SECONDS.
He pushes the tiny component. It slips. His jaw tightens. SEVEN SECONDS. He takes a ragged breath, refocusing, his eyes burning with fierce determination.
With a final, painstaking effort, his hand steadies. The gear drops perfectly into place. SIX SECONDS. A silent, desperate triumph.
He quickly, but carefully, closes the watch's delicate casing, snapping it shut with a barely audible CLICK. FIVE SECONDS. He holds the watch to his ear, listening.
Silence. The chimes of the grandfather clock crescendo. FOUR... THREE... TWO... ONE...
The final, deafening STRIKE of MIDNIGHT echoes through the room. A beat of profound, agonizing silence.
Then, a faint, rhythmic TICK-TOCK... TICK-TOCK... from the pocket watch. Soft, steady, perfect. Arthur's eyes, wide with disbelief and relief, well up.

A small, peaceful smile spreads across his face, pushing away the years of sorrow. He closes his eyes, pressing the watch to his heart. The New Year has arrived, bringing with it a quiet, longed-for peace.

Arthur must successfully repair the watch by midnight.

scene_end.turn: The watch ticks to life precisely as the grandfather clock strikes midnight, synchronizing with the New Year's arrival.

With each resounding chime of the grandfather clock, he races against time to secure a crucial, microscopic component, seeking a moment of peace and connection as the new year begins.

blocks.text:
INT. ARTHUR'S WORKSHOP - NIGHT (MOMENTS TO MIDNIGHT)

An elderly hand, lined with age and tremor, hovers over a workbench. ARTHUR FINCH (70s), a profound sorrow etched on his face, peers through a watchmaker's LOUPE. Before him, the delicate, open mechanism of his late wife's POCKET WATCH.
A majestic GRANDFATHER CLOCK dominates the background. Its ornate face clearly shows the time: TEN SECONDS TO MIDNIGHT. A low, resonant HUM begins to emanate from it.
Arthur holds a minuscule gear with fine-tipped TWEEZERS. His hand shakes, not just from age, but from the immense pressure, the emotional weight of this final, desperate act.
The GRANDFATHER CLOCK CHIMES, deep and sonorous, signaling the first second. Arthur flinches, a bead of sweat tracing a path down his temple. EIGHT SECONDS.
He pushes the tiny component. It slips. His jaw tightens. SEVEN SECONDS. He takes a ragged breath, refocusing, his eyes burning with fierce determination.
With a final, painstaking effort, his hand steadies. The gear drops perfectly into place. SIX SECONDS. A silent, desperate triumph.
He quickly, but carefully, closes the watch's delicate casing, snapping it shut with a barely audible CLICK. FIVE SECONDS. He holds the watch to his ear, listening.
Silence. The chimes of the grandfather clock crescendo. FOUR... THREE... TWO... ONE...
The final, deafening STRIKE of MIDNIGHT echoes through the room. A beat of profound, agonizing silence.
Then, a faint, rhythmic TICK-TOCK... TICK-TOCK... from the pocket watch. Soft, steady, perfect. Arthur's eyes, wide with disbelief and relief, well up.

A small, peaceful smile spreads across his face, pushing away the years of sorrow. He closes his eyes, pressing the watch to his heart. The New Year has arrived, bringing with it a quiet, longed-for peace.

blocks.text:
INT. ARTHUR'S WORKSHOP - NIGHT (MOMENTS TO MIDNIGHT)

An elderly hand, lined with age and tremor, hovers over a workbench. ARTHUR FINCH (70s), a profound sorrow etched on his face, peers through a watchmaker's LOUPE. Before him, the delicate, open mechanism of his late wife's POCKET WATCH.
A majestic GRANDFATHER CLOCK dominates the background. Its ornate face clearly shows the time: TEN SECONDS TO MIDNIGHT. A low, resonant HUM begins to emanate from it.
Arthur holds a minuscule gear with fine-tipped TWEEZERS. His hand shakes, not just from age, but from the immense pressure, the emotional weight of this final, desperate act.
The GRANDFATHER CLOCK CHIMES, deep and sonorous, signaling the first second. Arthur flinches, a bead of sweat tracing a path down his temple. EIGHT SECONDS.
He pushes the tiny component. It slips. His jaw tightens. SEVEN SECONDS. He takes a ragged breath, refocusing, his eyes burning with fierce determination.
With a final, painstaking effort, his hand steadies. The gear drops perfectly into place. SIX SECONDS. A silent, desperate triumph.
He quickly, but carefully, closes the watch's delicate casing, snapping it shut with a barely audible CLICK. FIVE SECONDS. He holds the watch to his ear, listening.
Silence. The chimes of the grandfather clock crescendo. FOUR... THREE... TWO... ONE...
The final, deafening STRIKE of MIDNIGHT echoes through the room. A beat of profound, agonizing silence.
Then, a faint, rhythmic TICK-TOCK... TICK-TOCK... from the pocket watch. Soft, steady, perfect. Arthur's eyes, wide with disbelief and relief, well up.

A small, peaceful smile spreads across his face, pushing away the years of sorrow. He closes his eyes, pressing the watch to his heart. The New Year has arrived, bringing with it a quiet, longed-for peace.

blocks.text:
INT. ARTHUR'S WORKSHOP - NIGHT (MOMENTS TO MIDNIGHT)

An elderly hand, lined with age and tremor, hovers over a workbench. ARTHUR FINCH (70s), a profound sorrow etched on his face, peers through a watchmaker's LOUPE. Before him, the delicate, open mechanism of his late wife's POCKET WATCH.
A majestic GRANDFATHER CLOCK dominates the background. Its ornate face clearly shows the time: TEN SECONDS TO MIDNIGHT. A low, resonant HUM begins to emanate from it.
Arthur holds a minuscule gear with fine-tipped TWEEZERS. His hand shakes, not just from age, but from the immense pressure, the emotional weight of this final, desperate act.
The GRANDFATHER CLOCK CHIMES, deep and sonorous, signaling the first second. Arthur flinches, a bead of sweat tracing a path down his temple. EIGHT SECONDS.
He pushes the tiny component. It slips. His jaw tightens. SEVEN SECONDS. He takes a ragged breath, refocusing, his eyes burning with fierce determination.
With a final, painstaking effort, his hand steadies. The gear drops perfectly into place. SIX SECONDS. A silent, desperate triumph.
He quickly, but carefully, closes the watch's delicate casing, snapping it shut with a barely audible CLICK. FIVE SECONDS. He holds the watch to his ear, listening.
Silence. The chimes of the grandfather clock crescendo. FOUR... THREE... TWO... ONE...
The final, deafening STRIKE of MIDNIGHT echoes through the room. A beat of profound, agonizing silence.
Then, a faint, rhythmic TICK-TOCK... TICK-TOCK... from the pocket watch. Soft, steady, perfect. Arthur's eyes, wide with disbelief and relief, well up.

A small, peaceful smile spreads across his face, pushing away the years of sorrow. He closes his eyes, pressing the watch to his heart. The New Year has arrived, bringing with it a quiet, longed-for peace.

blocks.text:
INT. ARTHUR'S WORKSHOP - NIGHT (MOMENTS TO MIDNIGHT)

An elderly hand, lined with age and tremor, hovers over a workbench. ARTHUR FINCH (70s), a profound sorrow etched on his face, peers through a watchmaker's LOUPE. Before him, the delicate, open mechanism of his late wife's POCKET WATCH.
A majestic GRANDFATHER CLOCK dominates the background. Its ornate face clearly shows the time: TEN SECONDS TO MIDNIGHT. A low, resonant HUM begins to emanate from it.
Arthur holds a minuscule gear with fine-tipped TWEEZERS. His hand shakes, not just from age, but from the immense pressure, the emotional weight of this final, desperate act.
The GRANDFATHER CLOCK CHIMES, deep and sonorous, signaling the first second. Arthur flinches, a bead of sweat tracing a path down his temple. EIGHT SECONDS.
He pushes the tiny component. It slips. His jaw tightens. SEVEN SECONDS. He takes a ragged breath, refocusing, his eyes burning with fierce determination.
With a final, painstaking effort, his hand steadies. The gear drops perfectly into place. SIX SECONDS. A silent, desperate triumph.
He quickly, but carefully, closes the watch's delicate casing, snapping it shut with a barely audible CLICK. FIVE SECONDS. He holds the watch to his ear, listening.
Silence. The chimes of the grandfather clock crescendo. FOUR... THREE... TWO... ONE...
The final, deafening STRIKE of MIDNIGHT echoes through the room. A beat of profound, agonizing silence.
Then, a faint, rhythmic TICK-TOCK... TICK-TOCK... from the pocket watch. Soft, steady, perfect. Arthur's eyes, wide with disbelief and relief, well up.

A small, peaceful smile spreads across his face, pushing away the years of sorrow. He closes his eyes, pressing the watch to his heart. The New Year has arrived, bringing with it a quiet, longed-for peace.
